import pandas as pd
import numpy as np
import seaborn as sns
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt

  2. Max food preparation time is 35 mins

Observation: 1) The highest number of orders which is around 160 orders take 24 mins to be delivered 2) The least number of orders which is around 40 orders take 31 mins to be delivered 3) Minimum delivery time is 15 mins 4) Maximum delivery time is 33 mins

  3. Vietnamese cuisine is the least popular cuisine to be ordered
  4. Number of orders are nearly same for both Indian and Mexican cuisines
  5. Number of orders are nearly same for Thai, Southern and French

Question 8: Which is the most popular cuisine on weekends? [1 mark]

In [56]:
most_popular_cuisine = (
    df.groupby(["day_of_the_week", "cuisine_type"])
      .size()
      .loc["Weekend"]
      .idxmax()
)

print("Most popular weekend cuisine:", most_popular_cuisine)

Most popular weekend cuisine: American

Observation: The most popular weekend cuisine is the American Cuisine

Question 9: What percentage of the orders cost more than 20 dollars? [2 marks]

In [57]:
# Total orders
total_orders = len(df)

# Orders with cost > 20
orders_above_20 = (df["cost_of_the_order"] > 20).sum()

# Percentage
percentage = (orders_above_20 / total_orders) * 100

print(f"Percentage of orders costing more than $20: {percentage:.2f}%")

Percentage of orders costing more than $20: 29.24%

Observations: 1) Nearly 30% of the orders cost more that \$20 2) 29.24\% is the exact percenatage of the orders costing more than $20. 3) About 30\% of the people ordering food from a restaurant spend more that \$20 dollars

Question 10: What is the mean order delivery time? [1 mark]

In [58]:
print(f"The mean order delivery time is {df['delivery_time'].mean():.2f} minutes")

The mean order delivery time is 24.16 minutes

Observations: 1) The average time taken to delivery food is 24.16 mins 2) Average number of customers wait for around 24 mins for their food to reach them from a restaurant after their food is prepared

Multivariate Analysis

Question 12: Perform a multivariate analysis to explore relationships between the important variables in the dataset. (It is a good idea to explore relations between numerical variables as well as relations between numerical and categorical variables) [10 marks]

In [61]:
cuisine_cost = (
    df.groupby("cuisine_type")["cost_of_the_order"]
      .mean()
      .sort_values()
)

plt.figure(figsize=(10,6))
sns.barplot(x=cuisine_cost.index, y=cuisine_cost.values, hue=cuisine_cost.index, palette="viridis")

plt.title("Average Cost of Orders by Cuisine")
plt.xlabel("Cuisine Type")
plt.ylabel("Average Cost ($)")
plt.xticks(rotation=45)
plt.show()

Observations:

  1. Vietnamese restaurant orders cost on an average the least which is about $12.5
  2. French restaurants cost the most with an average of \$19
  3. Japanese, Chinese and American cuisines on an average cost the same
In [62]:
numerical_col = ['cost_of_the_order','food_preparation_time','delivery_time']

plt.figure(figsize=(10,5))
sns.heatmap(df[numerical_col].corr(),annot=True,cmap='Spectral',vmin=-1,vmax=1)
plt.show()

Observations: 1) Each column has 100% correlation with itself 2) Cost_of_the_order and food_preparation_time has the most positive correlation 3) Delivery_time and cost_of_the_order has negative correlation

In [63]:
plt.figure(figsize=(10,5))
sns.scatterplot(data=df,x='cost_of_the_order',y='delivery_time',hue='cuisine_type')
plt.show()
In [64]:
plt.figure(figsize=(7,6))
sns.boxplot(data=df, x="delivery_time", hue="day_of_the_week")
plt.title("Delivery Time by Day of Week")
plt.show()

Observations:

  1. Min delivery time for weekday is 24 mins while for weekend is 15 mins
  2. Delivery time is faster during the weekends while slower during the weekdays
  3. There are no outliers in this graph
In [65]:
plt.figure(figsize=(8,6))
sns.boxplot(data=df, x="food_preparation_time", y="delivery_time", hue="day_of_the_week")
plt.title("Food Preparation vs Delivery Time by Day of Week")
plt.show()

Observations:

  1. Different cuisines have different delivery times
  2. Min delivery time during the weekdays is more 23 mins
  3. Min food preparation time during the weekends is 15 mins
In [66]:
plt.figure(figsize=(8,6))
sns.boxplot(data=df, x="food_preparation_time", hue="day_of_the_week")
plt.title("Food Preparation by Day of Week")
plt.show()

Observations:

  1. Food preparation time is the same during both the weekdays and weekends
  2. There are no outliers in this graph

  2. All cuisines have received ratings from 3 to 5

Question 13: The company wants to provide a promotional offer in the advertisement of the restaurants. The condition to get the offer is that the restaurants must have a rating count of more than 50 and the average rating should be greater than 4. Find the restaurants fulfilling the criteria to get the promotional offer. [3 marks]

In [68]:
rating_count = df.groupby('restaurant_name')['rating'].count()
In [69]:
df["rating"] = pd.to_numeric(df["rating"])
average_rating = df.groupby('restaurant_name')['rating'].mean()
In [70]:
restaurant_stats = pd.DataFrame({
    "rating_count": rating_count,
    "average_rating": average_rating
})

# Step 4: Filter restaurants
promo_restaurants = restaurant_stats[
    (restaurant_stats["rating_count"] > 50) & (restaurant_stats["average_rating"] > 4)
]

# Step 5: Print results
print("Restaurants eligible for promotional offer:")
print(promo_restaurants)

Restaurants eligible for promotional offer:
                           rating_count  average_rating
restaurant_name                                        
Blue Ribbon Fried Chicken            64        4.328125
Blue Ribbon Sushi                    73        4.219178
Shake Shack                         133        4.278195
The Meatball Shop                    84        4.511905

Observation:

  1. 4 restaurants are eligible for the promotional offer
  2. The Meatball Shop has the most average rating while Shake Shack has the most number of rating
  3. Blue Ribbon Fried Chicken has the least about of the ratings out of the 4 which is 64

Question 14: The company charges the restaurant 25% on the orders having cost greater than 20 dollars and 15% on the orders having cost greater than 5 dollars. Find the net revenue generated by the company across all orders. [3 marks]

In [71]:
revenue = 0
for x in df['cost_of_the_order']:
    if 5.0 < x < 20.0:
        revenue += x * 0.15
    elif x >= 20:
        revenue += x * 0.25

print("Total revenue generated by the company: $",revenue)

Total revenue generated by the company: $ 6166.302999999994

Observations: The net revenue generated by the company is more than \$6000

Question 15: The company wants to analyze the total time required to deliver the food. What percentage of orders take more than 60 minutes to get delivered from the time the order is placed? (The food has to be prepared and then delivered.) [2 marks]

In [72]:
total_time=df['food_preparation_time']+df['delivery_time']
c=0
count_of_orders=len(df['delivery_time'])
for x in total_time:
  if(x>60):
    c+=1

percentage_of_orders=(c/count_of_orders)*100
print(f"The percentage of orders that take more than 60 mins is {percentage_of_orders:.2f}%")

The percentage of orders that take more than 60 mins is 10.54%

Observations:

  1. Nearly 90\% of the orders take less than 60 mins to be delivered from the time the order has been placed
  2. Only about 10.5\% of the orders take more than 60 mins to reach the customers

Question 16: The company wants to analyze the delivery time of the orders on weekdays and weekends. How does the mean delivery time vary during weekdays and weekends? [2 marks]

In [73]:
# Calculate total time = prep + delivery
df["total_time"] = df["food_preparation_time"] + df["delivery_time"]

# Mean delivery time for weekdays
weekday_mean = df[df["day_of_the_week"] == "Weekday"]["total_time"].mean()

# Mean delivery time for weekends
weekend_mean = df[df["day_of_the_week"] == "Weekend"]["total_time"].mean()

print(f"The mean delivery time for weekdays is {weekday_mean:.2f} minutes")
print(f"The mean delivery time for weekends is {weekend_mean:.2f} minutes")

The mean delivery time for weekdays is 55.55 minutes
The mean delivery time for weekends is 49.91 minutes

Observations:

  1. Food takes more time to be delivered during the weekdays than the weekends
  2. Average delivery time during the weekends is about 50 mins

Conclusion and Recommendations

Question 17: What are your conclusions from the analysis? What recommendations would you like to share to help improve the business? (You can use cuisine type and feedback ratings to drive your business recommendations.) [6 marks]

Conclusions:

  1. Majority of the orders cost less than \$20, with the minimum around \$4.5 and maximum close to \$35.
  2. Percentage of orders costing more than $20: 29.24%
  3. Certain cuisines are cheaper like fast food, local snacks while others like continental or multi-cuisine tend to be more expensive.
  4. Mean delivery times differ between weekdays and weekends, with weekdays being slightly longer.
  5. Preparation + delivery times also vary by cuisine,some take significantly longer.

Recommendations:

  1. Restaurants and delivery partners can club multiple orders from the same or nearby localities into a single trip. This reduces delivery time, fuel costs, and traffic overhead while improving efficiency.
  2. Balance quality and affordability: cuisines that are both tasty and reasonably priced attract repeated customers.
  3. High-rated restaurants should maintain consistency; low-rated ones should analyze customer reviews for pain points
  4. Offer good deals to attract more orders.
